prompt:
Our internal plugin registry needs to resolve version constraints and we can't pull the npm semver package onto the build image (zero-dependency policy there), so I need a small semver.ts of our own. Exports:

parse(input) -> { major, minor, patch, prerelease, build } — numbers for the triple, prerelease as an array of dot-separated identifier strings ([] when absent), build as a string or null. Accept an optional leading 'v'. Throw TypeError on anything malformed: missing components, extra components, junk, and numeric components with leading zeros ('1.02.3' is NOT a version, the spec is clear on that).

compare(a, b) -> -1 | 0 | 1 using real semver precedence: compare the triple; a release outranks any prerelease of the same triple; prerelease identifiers compare pairwise where all-numeric identifiers compare as numbers, numeric ones rank below alphanumeric ones, and when one list is a prefix of the other the shorter sorts first. Build metadata is ignored entirely — 1.0.0+linux equals 1.0.0+darwin.

satisfies(version, range) -> boolean, where range is an exact version, ^x.y.z, or ~x.y.z. Get the caret zero-cases right: ^1.2.3 allows <2.0.0, ^0.2.3 allows <0.3.0, ^0.0.3 allows only 0.0.3-level patches (<0.0.4). Tilde pins the minor: ~1.2.3 -> >=1.2.3 <1.3.0. One npm behavior we rely on: a prerelease version can only satisfy a range whose base version has a prerelease AND the same major.minor.patch — 1.3.0-alpha must NOT satisfy ^1.2.0.

maxSatisfying(versions, range) -> the highest version string in the list that satisfies, or null.

Contract is test_semver.ts, run `node --test test_semver.ts`. Tests stay as-is.
